MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
G. Noel Kidd, Esq. 510-268-8600 of Kidd Carr LLP for Defendant.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT DAVID O'TOOLE, INDIVIDUALLY AND DOING BUSINESS AS DOT CONSTRUCTION'S Motion To Set Aside Default and Default Judgment.

Defendant David O'Toole's motion to set aside default and default judgment is GRANTED. O'Toole has satisfied the three-part Stiles test and thus is entitled to equitable relief on the ground of extrinsic mistake. (See Stiles v. Wallis (1983) 147 Cal.App.3d 1143, 1147-48.) (1) O'Toole's verified proposed answer establishes a "meritorious case" under Stiles. (Id.) (2) O'Toole has satisfactory excuses for failing to defend against the lawsuit: his confusion regarding summons papers due to five different lawsuits involving him and Nancy Wong; O'Toole's emotional and mental state; and the fact that an insurer's lawyer and a separate lawyer represented O'Toole, causing confusion over who would file his answer. (3) O'Toole acted diligently to seek a remedy upon discovery of the default judgment (mailing of notice of default is not dispositive of the time of discovery). In addition, answer is due by 12/19/18.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)
